rationale: The cited paper appears highly relevant to the citing paper’s theorized mechanism: both focus on numerical price perception (e.g., perceptual vs. conceptual/magnitude processing, perceived price differences/savings) and reference-price conditions. The citing note explicitly states it extends “reference-price and numerical-perception work,” which makes this citation plausibly foundational or mechanistic rather than incidental. With a direct DOI match (10.1177/0022243720932532), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
